content:
<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/minnetonka-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>

why is there a DEFINE FIELD directory ON agent_session TYPE string; ?

19 tool calls, 13 messages
It’s there to persist the coding agent’s working directory for each agent_session.

Session start requires directory in the API payload and passes it into session creation: mcp-route.ts:823, mcp-route.ts:831, mcp-route.ts:851.
The record is written with directory on agent_session: mcp-queries.ts:498, mcp-queries.ts:500.
It’s read back into MCP context (active_sessions) and returned to consumers: context-builder.ts:371, context-builder.ts:443, types.ts:81.
And because agent_session is SCHEMAFULL, any persisted key must be declared in schema: surreal-schema.surql:182, surreal-schema.surql:184.
58s
·


this makes no sense. an agent will work in multiple dirs

1 message
You’re right. A single directory on agent_session only captures one path and is a poor model if the agent moves across multiple dirs.

What it currently represents is effectively “start dir” (from sessions/start), not full working-dir history.

Better model:

Keep a root/start path (root_directory).
Track all touched dirs (working_directories: array<string> or a relation/event log).
Update session write paths to append/dedupe directories as work progresses.
If you want, I can implement this now with a new migration (0008_...) and update the MCP session APIs + context output accordingly.
21s
·


we already track all files changed

1 message
Agreed. If files_changed is reliably populated, agent_session.directory is redundant.

Only unique thing directory gives today is an immediate “current/start dir” during active sessions before sessions/end writes files_changed. If you don’t need that, we should remove it from:

agent_session schema/migration
sessions/start request validation/payload
active session context output/types
Then derive dirs from files_changed[*].path whenever needed.
11s
·


remove directory.

update existing migration.

Done. Removed `directory` from all 8 files:

- **Schema**: `surreal-schema.surql`, `0001_agent_session_and_subtask.surql` — field definition removed
- **Server**: `mcp-queries.ts` — removed from `createAgentSession` input/content, `mcp-route.ts` — removed from request type/validation/passthrough, `context-builder.ts` — removed from row type/query/mapping, `types.ts` — removed from `ActiveSessionContext`
- **CLI**: `http-client.ts` — removed from `sessionStart` param type, `system.ts` — removed from all 3 call sites + display format

Build is clean.
